req.title: Account close (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-039 defines three distinct partitions: (1) zero-balance accounts that close cleanly, (2) non-zero-balance accounts that are rejected without a sweep target, and (3) non-zero-balance accounts that close via sweep. Boundary values sit at exact 0.00, the smallest non-zero representable amount (0.01), and a maximum threshold. State transitions cover active→closed (success) and active→active (rejection). A decision table captures the sweep/no-sweep branching. Together these yield five test cases covering all mandatory heuristics with full traceability to R-FIN-039.

tests.name: test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds
expected: Response body contains final_statement URL or reference
trace: R-FIN-039: zero-balance account may close directly
R-FIN-039: expected outcome – final statement generated
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert "final_statement" in body
    assert body["final_statement"] is not None

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_no_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Account balance is 50.00 (non-zero, no sweep target provided)
expected: Response body contains error code indicating non-zero balance
trace: R-FIN-039: error pathway – 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
R-FIN-039: closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_no_sweep_returns_409(api_client, funded_account):
    account_id = funded_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert "balance" in body["error"].lower() or "sweep" in body["error"].lower()
    status_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_minimum_nonzero_balance_boundary_no_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Account balance is exactly 0.01 (minimum non-zero amount)
expected: Error references non-zero balance or required sweep
trace: R-FIN-039: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold (0.01)
R-FIN-039: error pathway – 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_minimum_nonzero_balance_boundary_no_sweep_returns_409(
    api_client, create_account_with_balance
):
    account = create_account_with_balance(balance="0.01")
    account_id = account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    status_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert status_resp.json()["state"] == "active"

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and destination accounts
Source account is ACTIVE with balance 150.00
Destination account is ACTIVE and owned by same customer
expected: Final statement reference present in response
trace: R-FIN-039: closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account
R-FIN-039: expected outcome – final statement generated
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds(
    api_client, funded_account, sweep_destination_account
):
    src_id = funded_account["id"]
    dst_id = sweep_destination_account["id"]
    pre_balance = sweep_destination_account["balance"]
    payload = {"sweep_to_account_id": dst_id}
    resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{src_id}/close", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert "final_statement" in body
    dst_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{dst_id}")
    new_balance = float(dst_resp.json()["balance"])
    assert new_balance == pre_balance + float(funded_account["balance"])

tests.name: test_close_account_active_to_closed_state_transition
preconditions: Account is in ACTIVE state with balance 0.00
actions: Attempt second POST /accounts/{id}/close on already-closed account
expected: First call returns HTTP 200 and state=closed
Second call returns HTTP 409 or HTTP 422 indicating account already closed
Account state remains closed after second attempt
trace: R-FIN-039: idempotency / re-close guard on CLOSED→CLOSED transition
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_active_to_closed_state_transition(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    first_resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert first_resp.status_code == 200
    assert first_resp.json()["state"] == "closed"
    second_resp = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close")
    assert second_resp.status_code in (409, 422)
    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "closed"
